title: Business-rule manager with proof of compliance — rule meta-schema, proof/trust model, enforcement seam
summary: Prior-art survey grounding decision #093 (a Plateau offering to author/version/govern/enforce business rules declaratively and emit an auditable proof they were applied). Two open standards answer different forks: DMN (OMG Decision Model and Notation) is the business-friendly rule meta-schema (decision tables + FEEL expression language) for authoring; OPA/Rego is the policy-as-code enforcement architecture (Policy Decision Point evaluates, Policy Enforcement Point enforces, decision logs record every verdict and why, replayable). Separate-and-decouple plus the standardize-the-meta-schema doctrine point at a new project adopting DMN rather than extending webvalidation (input invalidity is a different concern from domain policy). Proof-of-compliance is a known tamper-evidence ladder: hash chain (SHA-256 each record links the previous) to Merkle tree (O(log n) inclusion proofs) to transparency log (anchor the root externally), with blockchain as the heavy overkill end; the proof content is OPA-style decision logs anchored to webtraces spans, framed as OSCAL-aligned machine-readable compliance artifacts. Enforcement is the OPA PDP/PEP split observed at a trace point, runtime as the baseline proof-emitting venue. The divergent call is how far up the tamper-evidence ladder to go. Report: reports/2026-06-12-business-rule-proof-of-compliance.md.
